name: Albert Beckles
titles: Competed at the pro level into his 50s
why: Longevity and genetics icon nicknamed "The Ageless Wonder" for competing at an elite level for decades.

name: Arnold Schwarzenegger
why: The most culturally significant name in bodybuilding; his Encyclopedia remains a foundational training text.

name: Bev Francis
titles: Former world-record powerlifter turned elite bodybuilder
why: Pioneered a strength-first approach to women's bodybuilding and remains an active coach today.

name: Big Ramy
why: Modern mass-monster champion and the first Egyptian bodybuilder to win the Mr. Olympia.

name: Bill Pearl
why: Longevity and legacy icon who authored one of bodybuilding's best-selling training books.

name: Branch Warren
titles: Longtime IFBB Pro, Mr. Olympia top-5 finisher
why: Old-school-style heavy training documented extensively on camera at his own Metroflex Gym.

name: Brandon Curry
why: Modern Olympia champion known for being unusually articulate about his own training on camera.

name: Casey Viator
why: Directly tied to the HIT lineage through Arthur Jones's Colorado Experiment and later work with Mike Mentzer.

name: Chris Bumstead
why: Defines the modern Classic Physique division with one of bodybuilding's largest personal media followings.

name: Cory Everson
why: Most dominant women's bodybuilding champion of her era, undefeated across six Ms. Olympia titles.

name: Dennis Wolf
why: Modern European mass-monster representative of the 2000s-2010s era.

name: Dexter Jackson
why: Nicknamed "The Blade" for conditioning; the longevity record-holder for competing at the top level.

name: Dorian Yates
why: Redefined mass through Blood & Guts HIT at Temple Gym, building the most complete physique of the 1990s.

name: Flex Wheeler
titles: Widely considered among the most aesthetic physiques of the 1990s
why: Widely considered the most aesthetic physique of the 1990s; his memoir documents his career and health struggles.

name: Franco Columbu
why: Arnold Schwarzenegger's training partner and a two-time Mr. Olympia in his own right.

name: Frank Zane
why: Aesthetics-era icon whose Growth Program prioritized symmetry and proportion over sheer mass.

name: Greg Doucette
titles: IFBB Pro turned coach and YouTube educator
why: Modern, living, easiest-to-verify system built entirely around progressive overload.

name: Gunter Schlierkamp
why: European size representative of the late-1990s/2000s mass-monster era.

name: Hadi Choopan
why: Current-era champion known for a distinct training culture and nickname, "The Persian Wolf."

name: Iris Kyle
titles: 10× Ms. Olympia — most Olympia titles of any bodybuilder, any gender
why: Holds the record for most Olympia titles of any bodybuilder — male or female — in the sport's history.

name: Jay Cutler
why: High-volume Olympia-era champion known for an explicit refusal to train to failure.

name: Johnnie Jackson
why: Powerlifting-bodybuilding hybrid approach, well documented through heavy, verifiable competition lifts.

name: Kai Greene
why: Highly quotable modern philosophy built on mind-muscle connection, with one of bodybuilding's largest fan followings.

name: Kevin Levrone
why: Iconic 1990s mass-monster and frequent Olympia runner-up, later fronting his own signature supplement line.

name: Kim Chizevsky
why: Rare cross-division champion who won titles in both bodybuilding and fitness competition.

name: Larry Scott
why: Historically essential as the very first Mr. Olympia champion.

name: Lee Haney
why: Tied the record for most Mr. Olympia titles; his "stimulate, don't annihilate" philosophy prioritized longevity.

name: Lee Labrada
titles: IFBB Pro Bodybuilding Hall of Fame inductee
why: Symmetry-era icon who built a lasting education and supplement brand around his own training philosophy.

name: Lee Priest
why: 30+ year documented career; still filming his own training and commentary today.

name: Lenda Murray
why: Most-titled women's bodybuilding champion before Iris Kyle, dominant across the 1990s.

name: Lou Ferrigno
why: Crossover bodybuilding-to-Hollywood fame who still coaches and appears publicly today.

name: Markus Ruhl
why: Extreme-mass case study of the 2000s, with German representation at the top level.

name: Mike Matarazzo
why: Cult fan-favorite of the 1990s mass-monster era, known for his outsized personality.

name: Mike Mentzer
titles: Top-level Mr. America / Mr. Universe competitor
why: Defined High Intensity Training (HIT) as a rival philosophy to high-volume bodybuilding.

name: Nasser El Sonbaty
why: Huge 1990s frame paired with unusually articulate, multilingual magazine interviews.

name: Nick Walker
titles: Multiple-time Mr. Olympia top-5 finisher ("The Mutant")
why: Current-generation mass monster who films and narrates most of his own training sessions.

name: Paul Dillett
why: Iconic 1990s size and conditioning during a brief but memorable competitive career.

name: Phil Heath
why: "The Gift" whose physique became the showcase for coach Hany Rambod's FST-7 fascia-stretch system.

name: Rachel McLish
why: Founding icon of women's bodybuilding as the sport's first-ever Ms. Olympia champion.

name: Reg Park
why: Arnold Schwarzenegger's own idol and mentor, and an early strength-focused bodybuilding icon.

name: Rich Gaspari
why: Training philosophy spans two eras: competition-era "total annihilation" and a later Haney-taught recovery lesson.

name: Rich Piana
titles: Competed as an amateur and pro bodybuilder; major fitness-media figure
why: Hugely influential self-documentarian whose own YouTube back catalog remains a primary record of his training.

name: Robby Robinson
titles: Longtime IFBB Pro, decades-long competitive career
why: Longevity icon who remains active on social media and continues posing exhibitions today.

name: Ronnie Coleman
why: Most-titled male Mr. Olympia champion, pairing heavy compound lifts with genuine bodybuilding volume.

name: Samir Bannout
why: The "Lion of Lebanon" — the roster's most honestly thin-sourced champion, with no branded system.

name: Serge Nubret
titles: Decades-long competitive career across multiple federations
why: Unique high-volume, cardio-heavy training philosophy sustained across an unusually long career.

name: Sergio Oliva
why: Arnold Schwarzenegger's chief Golden Era rival, considered one of the greatest physiques of all time.

name: Shawn Ray
why: Outspoken 1990s contender who remains an active media personality today.

name: Shawn Rhoden
why: The most recent "classic-format" Olympia winner before the modern mass-monster era.

name: Steve Reeves
why: Foundational aesthetics icon whose career predates the Mr. Olympia contest entirely.

name: Tom Platz
why: Legendary leg-training specialist whose squat sessions remain a cult reference point in bodybuilding history.

name: Toney Freeman
why: Aesthetics-plus-longevity representative of the modern mass-monster era.

name: Victor Martinez
why: Strong 2000s-era physique and Dominican representation at the top level of pro bodybuilding.

name: Vince Gironda
why: Hugely influential pre-Olympia training theorist who ran his own Hollywood gym and mail-order courses.

name: Vince Taylor
why: Longevity and symmetry-era representative who won the Masters Olympia.
